steps.title: Find the passage's central focus
steps.content: The passage opens by introducing Fannie Barrier Williams and labeling her instrumental, then immediately illustrates her role with a concrete example. This tell-then-show structure signals that the main purpose is introducing and illustrating her work.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A states the main purpose as introducing and illustrating Barrier Williams's integral role in supporting Black women during the Great Migration — which the passage does by opening with her name and “instrumental” status, then showing her work securing job placements.

The other three either add unsupported details, treat context as the main focus, or confuse supporting information with the author's primary purpose.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
